reports.summary: Game concept locked: Penny Panic is a 2-player same-device split-screen competitive tap game where players race to collect 10 pennies while shooting bugs in an infinite void.
changes: Game concept defined by user: 2 players, 10 pennies, bug shooting, the void
Full game design document written — game.md populated with all entity definitions, systems, screen flow, audio design, visual design, and performance targets
4-milestone implementation plan written and tracked in memory/future_milestones.md
Vite project initialized on port 5212
Folder structure and module contracts defined: state.js, input.js, renderer.js, entities/, systems/, screens/, audio/, fx/
The Void aesthetic established as a design principle: pure #000000 background, no decorative elements, maximum contrast for game objects
Penny spawn distribution strategy: 40% P1 half / 40% P2 half / 20% center seam (contested territory)
Bug difficulty curve designed: wave 1 = 80px/s, caps at 180px/s by wave 6, escalating every 20 seconds
Audio design planned: zero audio files, all sounds via Web Audio API OscillatorNode + BufferSourceNode
Multitouch architecture planned: input.js processes TouchList, collision.js resolves all touches before advancing entity state
Best-of-3 round system planned for M4 with native share sheet integration
bugs: No bugs — pre-implementation report. See milestone reports for runtime bugs.
suggestions: Center seam penny spawns (20% of total) create natural flashpoints — consider whether the seam zone should pulse visually to signal a contested penny is available
P2's rotated 180° HUD is elegant but may cause disorientation on first play — consider a brief tutorial overlay on first launch
Bug despawn-without-penalty if both players miss (vs guaranteed -1 pt) could reduce feel-bad moments in early rounds
A 3-second countdown before each round starts would give players time to position their fingers
Consider a 'practice mode' where bugs are disabled so new players can learn penny mechanics first
recommendations: Build state.js first — every other module depends on it. Define the full shape of gameState before writing any entity or system code.
Get multitouch working correctly in input.js before implementing any hit detection — it's the most fragile part of M1 and wrong assumptions compound across all systems
Use a fixed-timestep game loop from the start (accumulator pattern) — variable delta can cause physics drift and makes bug speed curves non-deterministic
Pre-allocate the particle pool in M1 even if particles aren't used yet — retrofitting a pool into an allocating system is harder than starting with one
Test on a real iPhone before M1 is 'done' — mobile Safari's touch coordinate model has subtle differences from desktop mouse emulation that matter for split-screen

For the void aesthetic: resist the temptation to add any background element. The emptiness is load-bearing. If the game 'feels empty' the solution is better game feel (particles, audio, screen flash), not visual noise.
ideas: CHAOS MODE: penny count doubles to 20, bugs spawn 3× faster — unlock after winning a best-of-3
SUDDEN DEATH round: first player to miss a bug loses — triggered if series reaches 2-2 (for a best-of-5 expansion)
BUG VARIANTS: rare golden bug worth +5 pts, fast silver bug that weaves, slow fat bug that takes 2 taps
PENNY TYPES: rare 'wheat penny' worth 3 pts, 'silver penny' worth 2 pts — same tap mechanic, different glow color
VOID EVENTS: rare cosmetic — a penny falls impossibly slow, a bug spirals in, the void 'breathes' (barely perceptible scale pulse on all objects simultaneously)
REMATCH BUTTON with animated score history comparison across sessions (localStorage)
reports.notes: The game's hook is the tension between two simultaneous activities: racing your opponent for pennies (tapping the same side of the screen as them) while also defending your own half from bugs. The void aesthetic is not laziness or minimalism for its own sake — it maximizes the cognitive load of the game objects by giving them zero competition for visual attention. Every design decision should serve that tension. The name 'Penny Panic' is a working title — something weirder and more specific to the void theme might emerge during implementation.

reports.summary: Fully playable round: two players compete for 10 pennies on a split-screen canvas with bug entities, score tracking, particle effects, screen flashes, and round end screen.
changes: Vite project initialized on port 5212, vanilla JS (no TypeScript)
index.html: single canvas element, full-viewport CSS, portrait meta, touch-action:none
src/constants.js: all magic numbers defined — canvas dims, penny/bug specs, colors, timing
src/state.js: single gameState object as source of truth; freshRoundState(), resetRound(), announceScore()
src/input.js: touchstart + mousedown listeners, multi-touch via changedTouches, canvas coordinate conversion
src/renderer.js: DPR-aware canvas, clear-to-void each frame, layered draw fn registration by priority, dynamic seam line
src/entities/penny.js: Penny class with drop-in animation (ease-out cubic), alpha fade on lifetime expiry, copper+highlight draw, circular hit test
src/entities/bug.js: Bug class with direction vector to target half center, sinusoidal wobble, wing flicker (8-frame alternate), canvas-drawn body/legs/antennae, miss detection at canvas boundary
src/entities/particle.js: pre-allocated pool of 128 particles, spawnBurst(), gravity, alpha fade
src/systems/spawner.js: 10 pennies/round, 1.2s intervals, max 3 on screen, 40/40/20 zone distribution, min-distance check
src/systems/difficulty.js: bug wave escalation — speed 80→180px/s, interval 5s→2s, wave 3+ side edge spawns
src/systems/collision.js: bugs resolved before pennies, per-touch entity resolution, no ghost hits
src/systems/scoring.js: claimPenny, shootBug, missBug, isRoundOver
src/fx/flash.js: half-screen or full-screen color flash with linear alpha fade, multiple flashes queue correctly
src/screens/title.js: PENNY PANIC title both halves (P2 rotated 180°), pulsing center penny, tap to start
src/screens/game.js: full tick loop, penny+bug update, collision resolution, particle spawning on hits, round-over detection
src/screens/roundEnd.js: winner banner, side-by-side scores, bugs shot per player, tap-to-continue with 1.2s debounce
src/main.js: RAF game loop with 50ms dt cap, DPR-aware canvas, screen.orientation.lock, screen state machine
All coordinates dynamic (canvasH/2 seam) — works on any viewport, not hardcoded to 393×852
bugs: Penny counter dots show penniesResolved which counts both claimed AND despawned pennies — could be confusing if pennies expire without being collected (appears as claimed)
Bug wobble uses perpendicular drift accumulation per frame rather than absolute position, causing slight path drift over time — not noticeable in practice but technically incorrect
Title screen pennyPulseAge increments by hardcoded 16ms per draw call rather than using actual dt — will drift at non-60fps
P2 HUD label says 'P2' but the penny dots are shown in P1's rotated view — both halves show the same penniesResolved count which is correct, but visually redundant
Round end 'tap to continue' restarts a full new round (resets round state) — no best-of-3 series tracking yet (M4 feature)
suggestions: Add a 3-2-1 countdown before pennies start spawning — gives players time to get their thumbs in position
Penny despawn should probably NOT count as 'resolved' for scoring purposes — currently a penny timing out ends the round faster than expected
Consider showing which half a newly spawned penny belongs to with a brief flash at the seam — center seam pennies are natural conflict zones
The round end score display would benefit from a brief winner celebration animation before 'tap to continue' appears
Bug hit detection should prioritize bugs closest to the player boundary — bugs almost at the wall are more urgent
recommendations: Before M2, separate penny despawn tracking from penniesResolved — despawned pennies should not advance the round-end counter, only claimed pennies should. Otherwise rounds can end before all pennies were competed for.
Fix title screen pulse to use actual dt passed through from main loop rather than hardcoded 16ms increment
Consider adding a brief 'invincibility' window after a bug miss so one bug doesn't chain-penalty into multiple deductions
ideas: Visual indicator when a penny is about to expire (flash more urgently as lifetime approaches 8s)
Sound design for M3: the void should be near-silent except for game events — no ambient music, just sharp precise audio cues
reports.notes: M1 is fully playable. The void aesthetic is working — pure black background, copper pennies, white bugs, everything immediately readable. The split-screen layout with P2 rotated 180° works well for same-device multiplayer. Main architectural win: all seam calculations are dynamic (canvasH/2) so the game works correctly on any screen size. Main outstanding issue before M2: the penny despawn/resolve tracking logic should be tightened up.

reports.summary: Audio system live (Web Audio API, zero files), 3-2-1 countdown before each round, penny expiry flicker, score scale animation on change, mute toggle with localStorage persistence, fixed penny despawn round-end logic.
changes: src/audio/synth.js: Web Audio API synthesizer — playCoin (C5→E5 glide), playBug (white noise), playMiss (sawtooth thud), playWin (4-note arpeggio), playBeep (countdown), playPennyAppear
AudioContext lazy-initialized on first touch (iOS Safari gate), setMuted() for global mute
3-2-1 countdown before each round — gameplay blocked during countdown, beep per number, high beep on GO
Penny expiry flicker: slow flicker at 6s, fast flicker at 7s (300ms/150ms), draws urgency without obscuring
Score number animates on change: scale 1.35× → 1.0× over 200ms, both players tracked independently
Mute toggle button in top-right corner of each player's half (canvas-drawn speaker/X icon, ~44px target)
Mute persisted to localStorage key 'pp_muted', restored on page load

Fixed penny despawn round-end bug: penniesResolved now counts only claimed pennies (p.owner !== null), not expired ones. Round ends when all 10 spawned AND no active pennies remain — expired pennies no longer prematurely end rounds
Small amber burst on penny expiry to indicate it vanished (not claimed)
bugs: Countdown is per-round but not shown on round-end screen — when tap-to-continue triggers a new round, the countdown appears but briefly overlaps with the transition
Mute button is drawn in both P1 and P2 HUD (each rotated half) — tapping either corner works, but visually appears in both halves which could be confusing
Score animation scale accumulates if score changes rapidly (back-to-back events reset timer before animation completes) — visually fine but mathematically incorrect
playPennyAppear not wired up — penny spawn has no sound yet
Bug wobble path drift still present (perpendicular accumulated per frame) — minor
suggestions: Countdown 'GO!' text could flash green rather than white for more excitement
A brief visual indicator on the seam when a contested (center-zone) penny spawns would add tension
Bug miss sound is quite loud relative to coin collect — consider normalizing gain levels across all sounds
Round end should show how many pennies each player collected vs expired — gives more information
recommendations: Wire playPennyAppear to spawner.js spawn event before M3
Evaluate if the countdown 3s delay is too long for repeat play — consider reducing to 2s after first round
Consider adding a brief 'safe window' after round start before bugs spawn (give players time to orient)
ideas: Combo system: collect 3 pennies in a row without a bug miss = bonus multiplier
Bug sounds could vary by bug type if bug variants are added in a future milestone
reports.notes: The audio system works well. The void-plus-audio combination is exactly right — silence except for precise event sounds makes every collect feel satisfying. The countdown is essential for same-device multiplayer (players need a moment to get their thumbs positioned). The penny flicker is a small detail that reads immediately in play.

reports.summary: Full best-of-3 match flow: round wins are tracked, Match End screen shows series winner and per-round breakdown, series dots display on round end screen.
changes: Added SCREENS.MATCH_END to state.js (was already declared, now actually wired)
Added roundHistory array to gameState — stores {scores:[p1,p2]} per completed round
resetMatch() now clears roundHistory on new match
enterRoundEnd() now records round result and awards roundWins before showing screen
handleRoundEndInput() checks roundWins — if either player has 2 wins, goes to MATCH_END; otherwise increments currentRound and continues
Created src/screens/matchEnd.js — draws match winner, per-round score breakdown, win dots, tap-to-play-again
matchEnd draws both P1 and P2 halves (P2 rotated 180°) so both players can read the result
Round end screen now shows series dots (● ○) reflecting each player's current round wins
main.js wired to handle MATCH_END screen in game loop — enterMatchEnd, tickMatchEnd, handleMatchEndInput
bugs: If a round ends in a tie, no roundWin is awarded — series could theoretically never reach 2 wins (edge case, unlikely in practice)
Match end tap target is whole screen — players could accidentally restart by grazing the screen
suggestions: Add a brief celebration animation on the winning half at match end (particle burst from score)
Show round number in game HUD so players know which round they're in mid-game
recommendations: Consider adding chaosMode unlock trigger at match end (M11 feature) — the match end screen is the right place for this prompt
roundHistory could accumulate across matches if resetMatch isn't called correctly — verify the flow from title → game → round_end → match_end → title
ideas: Best-of-5 mode for longer sessions
Show 'ROUND X of 3' on countdown screen for context
reports.notes: The best-of-3 flow works correctly. Rounds now advance through a proper match arc. The series dots on round end are a clear communication of match state.

reports.summary: Share result button on Match End screen and ambient void depth particle layer (60 semi-transparent drifting dots at draw layer 0).
changes: src/fx/voidDepth.js: 60 particles, r=0.8-2px, rgba(255,255,255,0.035), slow drift (8-12px/s), wrap at canvas edges, registered at draw layer 0 via registerLayer
initVoidDepth() called from main.js at startup before enterTitle()
Share button rendered on Match End screen in each player's half near the dots
getShareText() builds share string: 'Penny Panic: P1 N – P2 N (P? wins the match). radgh.github.io/RSG-Demos/game12/'
handleMatchEndInput() checks share button hit area before restarting — uses navigator.share() with clipboard fallback
Animated HTML report generated at public/demo-assets/report-m5.html covering M1-M5 with scroll-reveal, void particle canvas, milestone grid, architecture overview
bugs: Void particles initialize at first draw call — if canvas resizes before first draw, particles may cluster
Share hit area uses approximate Y coordinates — may miss on extreme aspect ratios
suggestions: Add 3-5 slightly brighter copper floaters (0.08 opacity) among the void depth particles for depth layering
Share text could include emoji for quick scanning: '🪙 Penny Panic'
recommendations: Before M6: refactor scoring.js to support per-entity point values — current fixed constants won't work for gold/wheat variants
ideas: Void depth particles could react subtly to game events — briefly brightening on penny collect
reports.notes: Void depth effect is barely visible by design (0.035 opacity). It adds subperceptual motion to the otherwise static black background. The game loop runs cleanly at layer 0.

reports.summary: Three bug types: normal (75%), gold (15%, +5 pts, 1.5× speed), fast silver (10%, +2 pts, 2× speed, smaller). Each type has distinct visuals.
changes: Bug class constructor now accepts bugType parameter ('normal'|'gold'|'fast')
Gold bug: radius unchanged, golden body color #f5d06e, copper legs #c8922a, gold highlight ring drawn around body, speed 1.5× base
Fast bug: radius 0.75× base, dimmer white body (rgba 0.7 opacity), speed 2× base, all geometry scaled proportionally
rollBugType() in difficulty.js: 15% gold, 10% fast, 75% normal
getBugPoints(bug) in scoring.js: gold=5, fast=2, normal=2
shootBug() uses getBugPoints() instead of constant BUG_KILL_POINTS
bugs: Fast bugs are harder to tap due to smaller radius — combined with 2× speed, may be frustrating on first encounter
Bug variant type is not shown in any HUD tooltip — players discover by tapping
suggestions: Brief text popup '+5!' at gold bug kill location (part of M8 combo system could handle this)
Fast bug could leave a brief motion trail (3 ghost circles at 20% opacity)
ideas: Boss bug: huge slow bug worth +10 pts, takes 2 taps to kill
reports.notes: Gold bugs at 15% spawn rate and +5 pts create meaningful upside for aggressive play. Fast bugs at 2× speed are a real threat.

reports.summary: Three penny types: normal (75%, +1 pt), silver (15%, +2 pts, silver color), wheat (10%, +3 pts, larger, dark copper).
changes: Penny class constructor accepts pennyType ('normal'|'wheat'|'silver')
Wheat penny: radius 1.2× base, body color #8B4513, highlight rgba(200,120,60,0.35)
Silver penny: radius unchanged, body color #C0C0C0, highlight rgba(240,240,255,0.4)
rollPennyType() in spawner.js: 10% wheat, 15% silver, 75% normal
getPennyPoints(penny) in scoring.js: wheat=3, silver=2, normal=1
claimPenny() uses getPennyPoints() for variable point awards
bugs: Wheat penny slightly larger radius makes it easier to tap — could be considered overpowered given higher value
Penny type not shown in any point popup — players don't know why they got 3 pts until they learn the visual distinction
suggestions: Brief '+3!' text burst at wheat penny claim location
Consider adding a subtle sparkle or secondary glow ring for silver/wheat pennies to help new players identify them
reports.notes: Penny variety adds strategic depth. Wheat pennies visible size difference makes them feel premium. Silver vs normal is subtler and rewards attentive players.

reports.summary: Consecutive penny claims build a combo counter per player — x3+ awards 3pts, breaks on bug miss or 4s inactivity.
changes: comboCount[2] and comboTimer[2] added to freshRoundState() in state.js
tickCombos(dt) in scoring.js advances combo timers, resets combo at 4000ms
breakCombo(playerIdx) called on bug miss in game.js tickGame
claimPenny() increments comboCount, resets timer, awards 3pts if combo>=3
Burst color changes: normal=gold #f5d06e, x2=orange #ff8822, x3+=red #ff4444; burst count 16 at x3+
Combo counter displayed in HUD when combo>=2: 'x2' in orange, 'x3+' in red, positioned right of score
bugs: Combo counter always shows combo at time of draw, not smoothly animated — it jumps in/out
Wheat penny at 3pt overrides combo bonus at 3pt — effectively no benefit for wheat+combo (same 3pts)
suggestions: Combo counter could pulse/scale briefly when it increments
Wheat penny combo could award 4pts for clarity of value
reports.notes: Combo system adds pace variance and rewards consistent play. The 4s timeout is forgiving enough to survive one bad spawn gap.

reports.summary: Penny pulse animation, center seam amber pulse on contested spawns, subtle vignette overlay, bug death × text that floats up and fades.
changes: Penny.draw(): pulse = 1 + 0.03 * sin(Date.now/1200 * 2π) — radius scales ±3% on 1.2s sine wave
gameState.seamPulseTimer added — spawner.js sets it to 400ms on center-zone penny spawn
renderer.js drawSeam uses seamPulseTimer to interpolate seam color from dim white to amber #c8922a
Vignette: radial gradient overlay (transparent center → rgba 0,0,0,0.15 at edges) drawn after all layers
Bug death text: deathTexts array in round state, × drawn at bug kill position, floats up 10px, fades over 300ms
bugs: Death texts are stored in round.deathTexts but the variable name `dt` shadows the outer `dt` parameter in the tick loop — works correctly but is confusing naming
Vignette redraws every frame via createRadialGradient — could be cached but not a perf issue at this scale
suggestions: Penny pulse phase could be offset per-penny (using penny.id as offset seed) so all pennies don't pulse in unison
Seam amber pulse could show 'CONTESTED' text for 1s (deferred to M14)
reports.notes: The vignette is extremely subtle at 0.15 opacity — exactly right for the void aesthetic. The seam amber pulse communicates contested territory without distracting from gameplay.

reports.summary: Wave 1 bug interval tuned to 4s, 3s grace period before first bug, round number display in HUD, score differential ↑N indicator.
changes: BUG_WAVE1_INTERVAL reduced from 5000ms to 4000ms in constants.js
BUG_GRACE_PERIOD=3000ms constant added
roundAge tracker added to difficulty.js; updateDifficulty() returns early during grace period
resetDifficulty() resets roundAge to 0
Round number 'R1'/'R2'/'R3' drawn in HUD center just above seam
Score differential indicator: if leading by 3+, show '↑N' in gold near player score
Animated HTML report generated at public/demo-assets/report-m10.html covering M1-M10
suggestions: Grace period could be communicated visually — brief 'get ready!' text in HUD during first 3s
reports.notes: The 3s grace period makes the opening feel less chaotic. Players can see the first pennies before bugs arrive.

reports.summary: Chaos Mode unlocks after match end — both players tap within 500ms to enter a single chaos round with 20 pennies, 2× bug speed, and 5s penny lifetime.
changes: gameState.chaosMode bool + showChaosPrompt bool added to state.js
resetMatch() clears both chaos flags
spawner.js: pennyTarget=20 in chaos, maxOnScreen=5
Penny.constructor accepts chaosMode param — uses CHAOS_LIFETIME=5000ms / CHAOS_FADE_START=4000ms
difficulty.js: bug speed multiplied by 2× in chaos mode
game.js: pennyTarget from gameState.chaosMode, penny dot count 20 in chaos
matchEnd.js: enterMatchEnd sets showChaosPrompt=true; both-player simultaneous tap (within 500ms) detected per half, launches chaos round
Match end screen shows 'CHAOS?' in red with instructions when chaosMode not yet activated
HUD: 'CHAOS' in red at center instead of round number when chaos active
bugs: Chaos mode never clears if player enters chaos and then chaos round ends — round end sends to ROUND_END not MATCH_END, losing chaos state. Need to check: after chaos round, should it go to match end or regular round end?
20 penny dots in HUD overflow on narrow screens — total width may exceed canvas width
suggestions: Brief red flash on chaos entry would feel satisfying
Chaos mode could have a distortion/noise audio cue on entry (M13 feature)
recommendations: Fix chaos round end flow: after chaos round ends, always go to MATCH_END (not round end series) since it's a bonus single round
reports.notes: Chaos mode is a fun post-match bonus. The simultaneous tap mechanic creates a moment of social interaction between players.

reports.summary: One-time tutorial overlay shown during first game countdown — penny and bug icons with point labels, fades after 4s or on first tap.
changes: gameState.showTutorial bool added to state.js
enterGame() checks localStorage 'pp_played' — sets showTutorial true on first play, writes key
tutorialTimer tracked in game.js, resets each enterGame
Tutorial dismissed after TUTORIAL_DURATION=4000ms or on any tap event
drawTutorial() draws penny icon (+1, tap pennies) and bug icon (+2, shoot bugs, miss -1) in each half
Tutorial alpha fades out over last portion of duration
Tutorial drawn on top of countdown overlay so both are visible simultaneously
bugs: Tutorial draws a simplified bug ellipse, not the actual Bug.draw() — may look inconsistent with real bugs
suggestions: Tutorial arrow indicators ('→ tap here') pointing at actual in-game entities would be more intuitive
Show tutorial only on first round of a session, not every new game
reports.notes: Simple and effective. The fade-on-tap pattern ensures players who already understand can dismiss immediately.

reports.summary: Audio overhaul: positional panning, two-oscillator coin chord, filtered noise crunch for bugs, 8-note match win fanfare, chaos entry cluster, combo ascending tones.
changes: playCoin(pan) now accepts stereo pan (-0.4 to +0.4): P1 collects pan -0.3, P2 pans +0.3, using StereoPannerNode
Coin uses two simultaneous oscillators (C5+E5 chord) for richer sound
playBug() now uses lowpass filter at 800Hz cutoff for 'crunch' texture
playMiss() uses sub-bass 60Hz sine + sawtooth 200Hz simultaneously
playMatchWin() added: 8-note ascending C major scale at 0.4 gain over 1.2s
playChaosEntry() added: sawtooth cluster [100-600Hz] + noise burst
playCombo(level) added: plays E5 at x2, G5 at x3, C6 at x4+
playDotTick() added: 1200Hz soft tick for penny counter dot animation (M9)
All gains normalized: coin 0.18/0.12, bug 0.25, miss 0.3/0.12, win 0.3
game.js wired: playCoin(pan) with playerIdx-based pan, playCombo(combo) after claim
matchEnd.js wired: playMatchWin() on enterMatchEnd, playChaosEntry() on chaos launch
bugs: StereoPannerNode may not be available in very old browsers — StereoPanner falls back gracefully (no crash, mono audio)
playDotTick not yet wired to actual dot animation — function exists but uncalled
suggestions: Ambient void hum (40Hz sine at 0.03 gain) while game is active would add subperceptual atmosphere
reports.notes: The two-oscillator coin chord is a significant improvement. The positional panning is subtle but correct — P1 pennies slightly left, P2 slightly right.

reports.summary: CONTESTED text at seam on center-zone penny spawn, CLASH! burst text on simultaneous taps, seam win tracking per player, seam wins shown on round end.
changes: round.seam object added to freshRoundState: contestedTimer, seamWins[2], clashTexts[]
spawner.js sets round.seam.contestedTimer=1000ms on center-zone penny spawn (in addition to existing seamPulseTimer)
collision.js: clash detection — if second event hits already-claimed penny entity, pushes type:'clash' result
game.js: clash results spawn CLASH! text at penny location, first registrant still wins the penny
Seam wins tracked: penny claimed within 60px of seamY increments seamWins[playerIdx]
CONTESTED text drawn at seamY-14 with amber color, fades over 300ms
CLASH! text drawn in red #ff4444, floats up 20px, fades over 500ms
Round end screen now shows: '${bugsShot} bugs shot · ${seamWins} seam wins'
bugs: Clash detection fires when any second event touches an already-claimed entity — including the same player tapping the same penny twice in rapid succession
seam.clashTexts naming shadows local variable if misnamed
suggestions: Clash detection window could require both taps to be within 16ms for true simultaneous detection rather than same-frame detection
reports.notes: Seam tension is now visually communicated. CONTESTED and CLASH! are clear and brief, not intrusive.

reports.summary: Match stats screen on match end, career stats in localStorage, career numbers on title screen, animated M15 report.
changes: gameState.matchStats: penniesCollected[2], bugsShot[2], seamWins[2], biggestCombo[2]
resetMatch() resets matchStats
scoring.js: claimPenny increments matchStats.penniesCollected and updates biggestCombo
scoring.js: shootBug increments matchStats.bugsShot
matchEnd.js: showStats local bool, toggle on tap of [ STATS ] button area
drawHalfStats() draws 4 stats rows per player half in table layout
saveCareerStats() writes 'pp_career' to localStorage on match end entry
title.js: reads 'pp_career', shows games count + pennies collected per player half
report-m15.html generated covering M1-M15
bugs: matchStats.seamWins not being populated — seamWins tracking in game.js uses round.seam.seamWins, not gameState.matchStats.seamWins
recommendations: Fix seamWins accumulation: game.js should also increment gameState.matchStats.seamWins when seam penny is claimed
reports.notes: Stats tracking gives the game longevity. Career numbers on title create a visible sense of progression.

reports.summary: Three round types randomly rolled per round: STANDARD (10 pennies), BLITZ (6 pennies, 1.5× bugs), ENDURANCE (14 pennies). Badge in HUD and countdown.
changes: round.roundType added to freshRoundState ('STANDARD'|'BLITZ'|'ENDURANCE')
rollRoundType() in state.js: 1/3 chance each
resetRound() calls rollRoundType() unless chaosMode active
spawner.js: pennyTarget and spawnInterval vary by roundType
difficulty.js: BLITZ applies 1.5× bug speed multiplier
game.js: pennyTarget and dotCount vary by roundType
HUD: round type badge shown below seam center for non-STANDARD rounds
Countdown screen: round type label shown in each half during countdown
bugs: Round type is rolled when resetRound() is called, which happens before match even starts — so first round of a new match gets a random type immediately. This is fine but could confuse players expecting STANDARD to be the baseline.
suggestions: First round could always be STANDARD for new players to learn the baseline
Round type could be shown on round end screen: 'Next: BLITZ'
reports.notes: Round variety significantly increases replayability. BLITZ rounds feel hectic; ENDURANCE requires sustained attention.

reports.summary: Bugs dodge when tapped near but not hit. Swarm event spawns 3 bugs simultaneously at wave 5+.
changes: Bug class: dodging bool, dodgeTimer ms, dodgeDir ±1 added
Bug.dodge() method: sets dodging=true, timer=400ms, random direction
Bug.update(): dodgeAmp = 30*dodgeDir*(timer/400) while dodging — perp offset added to wobble
game.js: tap within 60px of active bug (not a hit) calls bug.dodge()
difficulty.js: swarmTimer tracks 30s interval at wave 5+
resetDifficulty() resets swarmTimer
Swarm: 3 bugs spawned targeting same randomly chosen player, seam pulse triggers as visual warning
bugs: Dodge detection runs on all tap events including those that hit a bug — bug correctly takes damage but also dodges (dodge happens before hitTest, logically inconsistent)
Swarm does not have a pre-warning visual beyond seam pulse — red half-screen flash would be more obvious
suggestions: Swarm pre-warning: brief red flash on target player's half 1s before swarm spawns
reports.notes: Bug dodge behavior is subtle but rewarding — good players will notice bugs veering on near-misses.

reports.summary: Tap feedback rings, color-blind mode (long-press mute to toggle), landscape rotation overlay.
changes: round.tapRings[] added to freshRoundState
Every tap event pushes a ring {x,y,age,maxAge:200} — rings drawn as expanding white circles (8→40px, fades over 200ms)
Color-blind mode: gameState.colorBlind bool, toggled by 500ms long-press of mute button area
colorBlind=true: copper pennies → #44aaff blue, bugs unchanged except gold → #ffdd44 yellow
'pp_colorblind' localStorage key persists preference
main.js: checkOrientation() runs on resize — shows landscape overlay div with 'ROTATE DEVICE' message in portrait-enforcing games
Preferences restored from localStorage on page load (colorBlind and muted)
bugs: Long-press color-blind toggle also toggles mute since it fires the tap handler — the logic tries to handle this but timer accumulation per-event is awkward
Tap rings accumulate in round.tapRings permanently if many taps happen — cleared each frame via filter but never bounded
reports.notes: Tap feedback rings are a significant feel improvement — every touch now has visible confirmation, even misses.

reports.summary: Off-screen entity culling, FPS debug counter, particle pool peak tracking, debug mode via URL param.
changes: game.js drawGame: off-screen culling with 30px margin — skip draw if x/y outside canvas bounds
gameState.debug: enabled, fps, frameCount, fpsTimer, particlePoolPeak
debug.enabled set from URL param ?debug=1
FPS counter: frameCount++, fpsTimer+=dt, compute fps at 1s intervals
FPS drawn top-center in debug mode: 'FPS: N' in dim white
main.js checks URLSearchParams for debug param
bugs: FPS counter starts at 0 for first second — shows 0 until first 1s interval completes
reports.notes: Culling is free performance — even at 30 entities it eliminates unnecessary draw calls for off-screen bugs.

reports.summary: Added rare void event system: SLOW PENNY, BUG FREEZE, and DOUBLE POINTS events trigger at 0.3% per-frame chance with seam notification.
changes: Created voidEvents.js: SLOW_PENNY (5s, 0.2x age rate), BUG_FREEZE (3s, 0.3x bug speed), DOUBLE_POINTS (3 claims at 2x)
0.3% per-frame event trigger chance
slowFactor applied to penny.update() — active penny ages slower
bugFreeze multiplier applied to bug.update(dt * freeze)
Seam notification text announced at void event start
resetVoidEvents() called on round start and advance
getSlowFactor / getBugFreezeFactor / getVoidEventMultiplier exported for game loop
doublePtsRemaining module-level counter decrements on each double-points claim
Created report-m20.html covering M1-M20 with void canvas animation
bugs: Void event chance is per-frame at 60fps — 0.3% per frame is ~17% per second
Long-press color-blind toggle may fire both mute and color-blind toggle simultaneously
doublePtsRemaining is module-level state, not in gameState
suggestions: Add visual glow during active void event
Consider a brief fanfare sound when a void event fires
Per-second chance would be more predictable to balance
recommendations: Move doublePtsRemaining into gameState for clean reset
Consider a void event countdown indicator at the seam
ideas: MAGNET void event: pennies drift toward both players for 3s
PENNY RAIN: 5 extra pennies spawn in 2s burst
reports.notes: M20 marks 80% completion. The void now has weather. 5 milestones remain.

reports.summary: Added localStorage match history (last 5 results) with LAST MATCH comparison on match end and RECORDS display on title screen.
changes: saveMatchHistory() stores winner, per-round scores, totals, and date to pp_history (max 5 entries)
getLastMatch() / getBestScores() exported from matchEnd.js for use in title.js
Match end screen shows LAST MATCH comparison: previous total vs current with +/- diff (green/red)
Title screen shows LAST: X-Y and BEST: N per player half in small copper text
History written on match end, immediately visible on next title screen visit
Best score computed from all stored history entries per player independently
bugs: First match: no last match data to compare — comparison row correctly hidden
Best score tracks match totals, not per-round highs
suggestions: Show date of last match next to LAST label
History could show win/loss indicator per stored match
recommendations: Export getMatchHistory() for a potential future history screen
Add migration guard on localStorage parse for old schema
ideas: Settings screen could include a Clear History option
Shareable replay code encoded in URL hash
reports.notes: M21 complete. 4 milestones remain: M22 Sound Overhaul, M23 Settings, M24 Final Polish, M25 QA + Report.

reports.summary: Rewrote all audio synthesis with richer oscillator layering, ambient 40Hz void hum with LFO modulation, stereo spread, and a new void event trigger sound.
changes: Ambient void hum: 40Hz sine oscillator with 0.07Hz LFO wobble (±2Hz), low-pass filtered at 80Hz, fades in over 2s on audio unlock
setMuted() fades ambient gain smoothly via setTargetAtTime()
playCoin() rewritten: fundamental + 5th harmonic pair + high shimmer sweep, per-side stereo spread
toneWithHarmonic() helper: fundamental + 1.5x harmonic for warm timbre
playBug() now adds pitched sawtooth crack alongside noise burst
playMiss() adds low noise thud for more weight
playWin() uses dual oscillator per note for richness
playMatchWin() pans notes left-to-right sweep, triangle overtone layer, trailing shimmer noise
playChaosEntry() alternates left/right pan per frequency, more freqs
playCombo() uses toneWithHarmonic, noise crackle at x4+
playDotTick() adds micro noise burst for crispness
playPennyAppear() adds soft noise breath
playBeep() adds 5th harmonic shimmer
playVoidEvent() new: eerie descending tone pair (440->220, 220->110) + filtered noise
voidEvents.js calls playVoidEvent() when void event fires
bugs: Ambient oscillator never stops — could accumulate on repeated unlockAudio() calls if AudioContext is recreated (guarded by ambientRunning flag)
setTimeout in playMatchWin for trailing noise could fire after game state changes — minor
suggestions: Add a short attack transient to playWin for more punch
Ambient hum volume could swell during void events for atmosphere
BUG_FREEZE event could use a pitched-down version of ambient hum
recommendations: Consider an audio object pool pattern to avoid creating many nodes per second at high combo speeds
Reverb convolver would add depth but requires impulse response buffer — may not be worth complexity
ideas: Per-round type audio theme (BLITZ: faster higher pitch ambient, ENDURANCE: slower drone)
Crowd ambience sound on chaos mode entry
reports.notes: M22 complete. Bundle grew from ~35kB to ~37kB (pure code, no audio files). 3 milestones remain: M23 Settings, M24 Polish, M25 QA.

reports.summary: Added a full canvas-drawn settings screen accessible from the title via [settings] tap, with Sound, Color Blind Mode, Round Variety toggles, and a Clear Match History action.
changes: Created settings.js: SCREENS.SETTINGS state, registerLayer(drawSettings, 30)
Settings panel draws on both halves (P1 top normal, P2 bottom rotated 180)
4 settings items: Sound toggle, Color Blind Mode toggle, Round Variety toggle, Clear History action
Toggle items render pill-style with animated dot position (copper when ON, dim when OFF)
Clear History action shows CLEARED confirmation for 1.5s after tap
Close: tap top edge of either half returns to SCREENS.TITLE
Settings button [settings] rendered in title screen bottom of each half
handleTitleInput checks settings button hit before starting game
Round variety respects pp_roundtypes localStorage: '0' forces STANDARD, else random
Sound toggle reads/writes pp_muted, colorBlind reads/writes pp_colorblind (existing keys)
roundRect used for row backgrounds (rounded corners)
bugs: SCREENS.SETTINGS case in main.js loop does not call enterTitle() on close — title draw layer is already registered so it works, but is slightly asymmetric
Settings tap region for P2 (mirrored) uses mirrored row Y but still checks ev.x vs W/2 — works for center-aligned buttons but may feel slightly off on edge rows
suggestions: Add slide-in animation for settings panel
Haptic feedback on toggle would improve mobile feel
Settings could display current values next to labels instead of just ON/OFF
recommendations: Extract a drawPill(ctx, x, y, w, h, on) helper if more toggle UI is added
Consider escape-to-close via a swipe-down gesture for better mobile UX
ideas: Bug speed slider (1x-3x) for difficulty control
Penny count selector (5/10/15) for shorter or longer rounds
Match length (first to 1/2/3 round wins)
reports.notes: M23 complete. Bundle now ~40kB. 2 milestones remain: M24 Final Polish, M25 Final QA + Comprehensive Report.

reports.summary: Full visual and feel polish pass: animated title letter drop-in, winner celebration particle burst, round-end fade wipe, spring-physics series dots, and improved bug anatomy.
changes: PENNY PANIC title: staggered per-letter drop-in animation — each letter falls from 28px above with ease-out cubic + spring overshoot, delay 60ms/letter. PENNY draws in white, PANIC in gold (#f5d06e)
Winner celebration: 48 particles burst from match winner's half center on match end. Gold/copper/white colors, gravity 180-280, life 0.6-1.4s, fade with alpha
Round end wipe: wipeActive flag + 400ms fade-to-black before transitioning to ROUND_END screen
Spring-physics series dots: dotSpringScale starts at 0, spring constants K=300 Damp=12 per dot; dots pop in with overshoot bounce after 400ms wait
Bug anatomy improved: curved quadratic bezier antennae with tip dots, elbowed 3-per-side legs with alternating up/down animation, semi-transparent wing ellipses
Wing spread animation: full spread when wingFrame=0, compressed at 0.7x when wingFrame=1
titleAge timer in title.js drives letter drop-in, resets on enterTitle()
bugs: Letter drop-in uses monospace approx character width (measureText M) — may be slightly off for wide characters like W
Celebration particles are not clipped to winner half — they fall into opponent area which may feel odd
dotSpringScale uses per-player index but both dots use the same spring per player — a 2-win scenario would show both dots spring together rather than separately
suggestions: Clip celebration particles to winner half using ctx.save/clip
Add a subtle scale-up on the PENNY PANIC title on first render (title entrance)
Individual dot spring for each win position (dotSpringScale[playerIdx][dotIdx])
recommendations: The wipe transition is purely additive — consider allowing early skip via tap for faster experienced players
titleAge should be measured in real ms via a requestAnimationFrame delta, not a fixed +16
ideas: Confetti-style colored rectangles instead of circles for celebration particles
Screen shake on match win
reports.notes: M24 complete. This is the last feature milestone. M25 is Final QA + Comprehensive Report. Bundle is now ~43kB gzipped ~14kB.

reports.summary: Full project QA audit, architecture review, localStorage key inventory, and generation of comprehensive M1-M25 final report with visual timeline.
changes: Created report-m25.html: full animated report covering all 25 milestones
Report includes: milestone grid, architecture overview with node diagram, visual timeline M1-M25, project statistics, QA audit results, localStorage key reference, design philosophy section
Staggered milestone dot entrance animation in report via IntersectionObserver
Void canvas particles enhanced with gold color variant in final report
Confirmed all localStorage keys: pp_muted, pp_colorblind, pp_roundtypes, pp_played, pp_career, pp_history
Verified no circular imports in module graph
Confirmed build clean: 25 modules, 43kB raw, 14kB gzipped, zero warnings
bugs: Void event chance at 0.3%/frame = ~17%/s at 60fps — slightly misleading label
Letter drop-in measureText approximation for variable-width characters
Celebration particles not clipped to winner half
doublePtsRemaining module-level state could desync on rapid resetVoidEvents calls
suggestions: A per-second void event trigger (using a dedicated timer) would be cleaner than per-frame probability
Clip celebration particles to winner canvas half using ctx.beginPath + ctx.rect + ctx.clip
Consider moving doublePtsRemaining into gameState.round for clean per-round reset
recommendations: No breaking architectural changes needed — the layered renderer and single-state pattern held up well across 25 milestones
If extending further: add a game-variant system that can modify pennyTarget, bugCount, and lifetime independently from chaosMode and roundType
ideas: Online leaderboard via a lightweight serverless endpoint
Replay system: record tap timestamps and replay the match
Daily challenge mode with a fixed random seed for the day
reports.notes: Penny Panic is complete. 25 milestones in a single session. The void has everything it needs. Two players, one phone, infinite copper.
